discrepancies.description: Relay 049 §TASK header: 'Per W19 SYNTH-SPEC (verbatim from 045 substrate §7 and departing-Synthesizer's note): "Right framing for that turn is: which option preserves the MOST of the manuscript substance."' The cited §7 of 045 substrate (siarc-relay-bridge/sessions/2026-05-08/P008-INPUT-PACKAGE-FOR-MSB-2026-06/p008_input_package_for_msb_2026-06.md, L484-528) is titled 'M6 \u2705-vs-Phase-A/B.5 status' and contains M6 arbitration grounding -- not P11 framing. The actual quote ('The right framing for that turn is: **which option preserves the most of the manuscript's structural contribution while honestly addressing the four fatals**...') is at cli_log/2026-05-05.md L687-689, inside the synth-comment block timestamped ~13:55 JST.
discrepancies.operator_action: Cosmetic. The framing intent is preserved (and grounded at cli_log/2026-05-05.md L687-689); only the citation pointer was wrong. Not a halt trigger -- recorded under rule5 discipline (no absolute claim survives without grounding to its actual source). Future relay drafters should cite the cli_log source directly.

discrepancies.description: Relay 049 §PRECONDITIONS P2 cites 'sessions/2026-05-04/P11-SICF-ARBITRATOR/handoff.md' as the SICF arbitrator handoff source. No such directory or file exists in the bridge. Same discrepancy logged in 047 as D2_sicf_arbitrator_path_notional. The actual SICF arbitrator output is the SICF run summary in cli_log/2026-05-05.md L580-636 (CLI's writeup of the four-fatals + four realistic options, output of sicf-review-system run with verdict Withdraw_and_resubmit, advocate=8.6, critic=4.0, consensus=6.22, sha256 b3b339507ec0337e7217d426cacf0c64a9c94c37192ff76b813f6a3b750b3453) plus the synth-comment block at L660-689 (synth's read on the four fatals + framing for the eventual strategic call). The Strategyzer handoff (sessions/2026-05-08/RACI-V2026-05-08-INSTALL/STRATEGYZER_HANDOFF_2026-05-08.md \u00a7A.2) summarizes the verdict but explicitly states 'Four strategic options were flagged but their content was not pasted to me; CLI has them in SQL' -- so the four options enumeration itself only exists at cli_log/2026-05-05.md L613-636.
discrepancies.operator_action: Operator confirmed via vscode_askQuestions on 2026-05-06 that cli_log/2026-05-05.md L580-636+L660-689 may be treated as the P2 substrate. Halt is on P3 (JTNB verdict awaited), not P2; this discrepancy is informational. Future relay drafters should cite cli_log source directly when referring to P11 SICF arbitrator output, until/unless the arbitrator output is migrated to a canonical bridge path (e.g., sessions/2026-05-05/P11-SICF-ARBITRATOR/handoff.md backfill).

discrepancies.description: Three substrate sources disagree on P11's current venue: (a) cli_log/2026-05-05.md L606 says 'P11 (F(2,4) base case) is currently \u0060under_review\u0060 at Math of Comp', (b) the four-options block at L613-636 references both Math of Comp (options 1-3) and JTNB-2400 (option 4 'Hold for JTNB-2400 leverage. The same content overlaps with the JTNB submission'), suggesting two parallel submissions, (c) CMB.txt L24/L59-60 + bridge submission_log entry (CMB L877-878) clearly show P11 was submitted to JTNB on 2026-04-28 (ID: JTNB-2400, Adamczewski) after a 2026-04-28 desk reject from Math.Comp (CMB L739-758). 047 handoff resolves this drift (\u00a7Anomalies #6: 'pre-existing labelling drift in the master prompt') and treats P11 as JTNB now / Math.Comp. as defensive pivot.
discrepancies.operator_action: Already resolved at 047. Carrying into 049 record because the four-options enumeration in cli_log L613-636 was authored under the labelling drift -- when the JTNB verdict lands and 049 re-fires, the option-by-option scoring (especially option 4 'Hold for JTNB-2400 leverage') needs venue-corrected re-interpretation. Specifically: under the corrected view, all four options are about the JTNB submission, so option 4 must be re-stated (e.g., 'Hold for companion-paper leverage' or merged with option 1). Synthesizer review recommended at re-fire time.
